trial_identity.brief_title: BRiTE - Bispecific T Cell Engager for Patients With Glioblastoma
trial_identity.official_title: A Phase 1 Study of Bispecific T Cell Engager (BRiTE) in Patients With Newly Diagnosed or Recurrent Glioblastoma
trial_identity.sponsor: Mustafa Khasraw, MBChB, MD, FRCP, FRACP

asset_identity.sponsor: Mustafa Khasraw, MBChB, MD, FRCP, FRACP

secondary_endpoint_measures: Pharmacokinetic (PK) of BRiTE observed during Cycle 1 of BRiTE infusion
trial_design_features.eligibility_summary: Inclusion Criteria: 1. Age ≥ 18 years old at the time of entry into the study 2. For both newly diagnosed and recurrent patients, pathologically documented supratentorial WHO grade 4 GBM with a confirmed EGFRvIII mutation at the most recent diagnosis, with radiographic contrast enhancing disease that is ≤ 4 cm in maximal diameter in any plane before resection. i. ndGBM patients: Must have undergone surgical resection of their tumor. Patients are still eligible if they have measurable or non-meas

risk_factors: glioblastoma indication is high-credibility-sensitive and endpoint interpretation may be confounded by surrogate biomarkers
primary endpoint is DLT; secondary ORR/PK are exploratory and not efficacy-confirmatory
endpoint_risk_assessment.rationale: BRiTE is a phase 1, single-arm, single-site glioblastoma study with a primary safety/tolerability endpoint (DLT) and small estimated enrollment. The intervention is a CD3-engaging bispecific antibody in a neuro-oncology setting, which increases operational and interpretive risk. ORR and PK are secondary/exploratory, so there is limited basis for endpoint credibility beyond early safety assessment. Comparator trials in the same disease/phase are also mostly small early-phase studies, reinforcing a high-uncertainty development context.

missing_data_flags.reason: No comparator/control arm is listed; single-arm interpretation limits credibility of non-safety endpoints.
No full protocol definition beyond DLT is provided for dose-escalation rules, stopping criteria, or cohort schema.
No RxNorm match was available for the intervention, reducing normalization confidence.

enrollment_duration_risk.rationale: Small single-site phase 1 study (US only) with 18 estimated participants over ~16 months. Eligibility is restrictive: EGFRvIII-confirmed supratentorial GBM, KPS ≥70, MRI feasible, lab thresholds, caregiver availability, and additional treatment-timing rules differ for newly diagnosed vs recurrent patients. Operational burden is high due to infusion visits, cycle-based monitoring, and caregiver requirements.

comparator_benchmarking.benchmark_summary: ClinicalTrials.gov search found 10 public comparator trials for Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma and PHASE1. Only NCT07343986 is a relevant mechanistic comparator; the others are GBM phase 1 studies but are modality/backbone mismatches and should be treated as excluded comparators.
comparator_benchmarking.landscape_summary: Found 10 ClinicalTrials.gov records for Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma.
comparator_benchmarking.sponsor_summary: CANbridge Life Sciences Ltd.: 1; Celgene: 1; Duke University: 1; Eli Lilly and Company: 1; Mundipharma Research Limited: 1; National Cancer Institute (NCI): 1; Sidney Kimmel Comprehensive Cancer Center at Johns Hopkins: 1; University of Virginia: 1; Washington University School of Medicine: 2
comparator_benchmarking.endpoint_summary: 25 primary endpoints were normalized across 10 trials.

likely_failure_modes.rationale: Small, single-site, restrictive phase 1 study with a narrow EGFRvIII-confirmed GBM population and caregiver/MRI/RT timing requirements.
Mechanism is highly specific (EGFRvIII/CD3 bispecific T-cell engager) in glioblastoma, so target expression/heterogeneity and tumor microenvironment could limit activity.
Primary endpoint is DLT for a CD3-engaging immunotherapy, which carries meaningful early tolerability risk in neuro-oncology.
Treatment logistics are complex: infusion-based cycles, mandatory Day 4 visits/caregiver presence, MRI eligibility, and tightly sequenced prior-therapy rules.
No control arm, no posted results, and limited protocol-level details on dose-escalation/stopping rules reduce interpretability and external benchmarking.

safety_context.summary: OpenFDA label context indicates only "WARNINGS FOR EXTERNAL USE ONLY" for BRiTE; no additional label-derived safety details were supplied.

missing_data_flags.reason: Only a generic external-use warning was provided; no drug-specific contraindications, boxed warnings, adverse reactions, or precautions were supplied.

label_expansion_clinical_rationale.rationale: Registry condition context (Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma, Recurrent Glioblastoma) can be compared with public label indications for clinical expansion rationale. Label indication context: USES: HELPS PREVENT SUNBURN. IF USED AS DIRECTED WITH OTHER SUN PROTECTION MEASURES (SEE DIRECTIONS), DECREASES THE RISK OF SKIN CANCER AND EARLY SKIN AGING CAUSED BY THE SUN

claims.claim_text: NCT04903795 has ClinicalTrials.gov status NOT_YET_RECRUITING and phases PHASE1.
NCT04903795 reports enrollment 18 and 1 primary endpoints.
Endpoint risk level is medium based on registry primary endpoint structure.
Enrollment and duration risk level is high.
ClinicalTrials.gov comparator benchmarking matched 10 public trials.
Failure-mode classification overall risk level is high.
The inferred clinical asset for NCT04903795 is hEGFRvIII-CD3 (BRiTE).
Historical PoS estimate is 0.053 for Oncology Phase I.
Approval likelihood proxy is 0.053 using the source-backed historical PoS basis.
Public label safety context was found for the asset or close label term.
Clinical label-expansion rationale was structured from registry condition context and available public label context.
